title: Human Development Index groups
subtitle: The Human Development Index (HDI) is a summary measure of key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, a good education, and a decent standard of living. The groups below define different levels of human development depending on the HDI values.
caption: Across the globe in 2023, countries are classified into four Human Development Index groups based on health, education, and living standards. The map displays a distinct geographic distribution of these development levels, with the "Very high" category covering North America, most of Europe, Australia, and parts of East Asia. The "High" group includes nations in South America, such as Argentina and Chile, alongside Russia and China. The "Medium" classification appears across much of Southeast Asia, India, and parts of North Africa. The "Low" group is concentrated in a large contiguous band across sub-Saharan Africa, including countries like Niger, Chad, and the Central African Republic, as well as Yemen and Afghanistan. Greenland and Somalia are marked as having no data. The highest development levels cluster in the northern hemisphere and Oceania, while the lowest levels are found primarily in central and western Africa.
